stories.headline: Agility Robotics to Go Public in $2.5 Billion SPAC Deal, a First for Humanoid Robots
stories.key_takeaway: Agility Robotics' $2.5 billion SPAC merger with Michael Klein's Churchill Capital Corp XI would make it the first US-listed pure-play humanoid-robotics company, backed by real commercial deployments of its Digit robot.

pull_quote.text: Agility is a humanoid first mover with proven technology, real-world deployments, and the trust of some of the world's most demanding enterprises.
pull_quote.role: Chairman and CEO, Churchill Capital Corp XI

data_points.label: Hours Digit has logged in live production

stories.article_excerpt: Agility Robotics said it will go public through a $2.5 billion merger with Churchill Capital Corp XI, a bet that a proven factory robot can win over public markets.

stories.headline: Chevron and Microsoft Sign 20-Year Deal to Power a Giant West Texas AI Data Center With Natural Gas
stories.key_takeaway: Chevron and Microsoft's Project Kilby will build a dedicated 2.67-gigawatt natural-gas plant beside a West Texas AI campus, sidestepping the multi-year grid queues that have become the biggest bottleneck in the AI buildout.

pull_quote.text: AI is reshaping the global economy, and abundant, affordable, reliable energy is essential to fueling that transformation.

data_points.label: Dedicated capacity for the Microsoft campus
Projected state and local tax revenue
Jobs the project is expected to support

stories.article_excerpt: Chevron and Microsoft signed a 20-year deal to build a dedicated 2.67-gigawatt gas plant beside a new AI campus near Pecos, Texas, and skip the grid entirely.

stories.headline: Tesla Tapes Out Its AI5 Chip, Betting on U.S.-Made Silicon for Optimus and Robotaxis
stories.key_takeaway: Tesla froze the design of its AI5 inference chip and sent it to TSMC (Arizona/N3) and Samsung (Texas) for dual-sourced production, delivering roughly 5x the compute of its current dual-AI4 setup and prioritizing Optimus and training clusters over cars.

pull_quote.text: A single AI5 has ~5 times the useful compute of a dual SoC AI4.

data_points.label: First engineering wafers due at Palo Alto lab

stories.article_excerpt: Tesla has taped out its next-generation AI5 inference chip, sending finalized designs to TSMC and Samsung and pinning its robots and robotaxis on silicon it designed itself.

stories.headline: SoundHound AI to Acquire LivePerson, Fusing Voice AI With Enterprise Messaging
stories.key_takeaway: SoundHound AI is buying LivePerson in an all-stock deal that pairs its voice and agentic AI with LivePerson's Conversational Cloud - about one billion customer messages a month - to build an end-to-end omnichannel platform it says opens a $500M revenue opportunity.

pull_quote.text: The artificial boundaries between 'talking' and 'typing' are disappearing. Consumers expect to start a complex request over the phone and finish it seamlessly via text or web messaging, without ever repeating themselves or losing context.

data_points.label: customer messages per month on LivePerson's Conversational Cloud
revenue opportunity from the combined base
global banks among the combined footprint

stories.article_excerpt: SoundHound AI is acquiring LivePerson in an all-stock deal that fuses voice AI with a messaging platform carrying a billion customer messages a month.

stories.headline: Cohere and Aleph Alpha to Merge, Forming a Transatlantic Sovereign-AI Champion
stories.key_takeaway: Cohere is merging with Germany's Aleph Alpha in a roughly $20 billion deal anchored by EUR 500 million from the Schwarz Group, betting that European governments and regulated industries will pay for AI that keeps their data out of US jurisdiction.

pull_quote.text: Combining the strengths of Cohere and Aleph Alpha accelerates our global expansion and advances our mission to deliver sovereign AI to nations around the world.

stories.article_excerpt: Canada's Cohere and Germany's Aleph Alpha are combining into a roughly $20 billion company aimed squarely at governments that want AI under domestic control.

stories.headline: OpenAI's GeneBench-Pro Exposes How Far AI Still Is From Real Biology Research
stories.key_takeaway: OpenAI's new GeneBench-Pro benchmark shows frontier AI models still fail roughly 70% of research-grade computational biology problems, exposing a persistent gap in scientific judgment even as benchmarks become the industry's newest competitive battleground.

pull_quote.text: They aren't cautious enough about data issues. Maybe that highlights a weakness of current models. And a lot of biological data has irregularities.

data_points.label: GPT-5.6 Sol's pass rate (31.5% in Pro mode)
time a human expert needs per problem
Claude Opus 4.8's score, best among non-OpenAI models

stories.article_excerpt: OpenAI's GeneBench-Pro hands AI agents the messy work real computational biologists do daily - and even its best model solves fewer than one in three problems.

stories.headline: Moonshot's Kimi K2.7 Code Sharpens China's Open-Weight Push Into Agentic Coding
stories.key_takeaway: Moonshot AI's open-weight Kimi K2.7 Code posts first-party coding gains over K2.6 and undercuts closed US frontier models on price, deepening a wave of cheap Chinese open-weight releases now competing at the top of coding leaderboards.

pull_quote.text: Kimi K2.7 Code cuts thinking tokens 30% - but practitioners say the benchmarks don't check out.

stories.article_excerpt: Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.7 Code lands as a coding-specialized, open-weight follow-up to K2.6 - cheaper reasoning, agentic tool use, and a Modified MIT license.

stories.headline: DeepSeek V4 Pro Tops the Open-Weight Coding Charts, Closing the Gap to Closed Frontier Models
stories.key_takeaway: DeepSeek's V4 Pro is now the leading open-weight model on coding benchmarks - roughly 80.6% on SWE-bench Verified and 93.5% on LiveCodeBench - at a fraction of closed-frontier API prices, sharpening the US-China AI cost dynamic.

pull_quote.text: When frontier-adjacent AI becomes predictably cheap and predictably open, the economics of the entire industry shift.

data_points.label: Cheaper than GPT-5.5 per output token

stories.article_excerpt: DeepSeek's V4 Pro has become the top open-weights model on the coding benchmarks developers watch - at a fraction of the cost of the closed frontier.

stories.headline: A 10-Second EKG and AI: Michigan Researchers Target a Heart Condition That Hides From Standard Tests
stories.key_takeaway: University of Michigan researchers built a self-supervised AI model that detects coronary microvascular dysfunction - often missed in women - from a standard 10-second EKG rather than costly PET imaging, hitting an AUROC of 0.763 for impaired myocardial flow reserve.

pull_quote.text: People who come to the ER for chest pain might have CMVD, but their angiogram will show up as 'clear.' In hospitals with limited resources or non-specialty centers, using our EKG-AI model will be an easy, cost-effective and non-invasive way to identify when a patient would benefit from advanced testing.
pull_quote.role: Associate professor of internal medicine-cardiology, U-M Medical School

data_points.label: unlabeled EKG waveforms used to pretrain the model
AUROC for detecting impaired myocardial flow reserve
length of the EKG strip the model needs
annual U.S. ER/outpatient visits for chest pain

stories.article_excerpt: A hard-to-detect heart condition that standard imaging routinely misses could be flagged from a single 10-second EKG strip, Michigan researchers say.

stories.headline: An AI Called DuctGPT Is Hunting for Magnets That Don't Need China's Rare Earths
stories.key_takeaway: Ames National Laboratory scientist Prashant Singh is extending DuctGPT - a physics-informed generative AI first built for fusion alloys - into a workflow that screens candidate rare-earth-free permanent magnets, part of a DOE push to cut US dependence on China-dominated critical minerals.

pull_quote.text: Once you understand the physics of what controls specific properties, then you and your agentic tools or AI frameworks can search arbitrary material space.

data_points.label: Discovery time DuctGPT aims to compress
How long researchers have hunted rare-earth-free magnets
Elements China added to export controls in April 2025
U.S. DoD equity stake in MP Materials (July 2025)

stories.article_excerpt: A physics-trained AI first built for fusion reactors is being turned loose on finding powerful permanent magnets that don't depend on China's rare earths.

stories.headline: Coding Agents Can Now Build an AlphaZero Pipeline From Scratch, New Research Shows
stories.key_takeaway: Given only a short task description and three hours on a consumer PC, a frontier coding agent (Claude Opus 4.7) autonomously built a full AlphaZero-style self-play pipeline for Connect Four whose trained agent matched or beat the Pascal Pons solver in half its runs - a task no frontier agent could reliably do months earlier.

pull_quote.text: The task, which no frontier agent could reliably complete when we began development in January of 2026, is now near-saturation.

data_points.label: Opus 4.7 first-mover wins vs. the Pons solver (others <=2 of 8)
Time budget on a single consumer GPU per trial

stories.article_excerpt: Give a frontier coding agent a paragraph of instructions and three hours, and it will now build a working AlphaZero self-play pipeline from an empty directory.

stories.headline: Five Eyes Agencies Issue Joint Guidance on Securing Agentic AI in Critical Infrastructure

sources.name: CISA - Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services

stories.key_takeaway: The Five Eyes cyber agencies issued their first joint security guidance on agentic AI, cataloguing 23 risks across five categories and urging critical-infrastructure operators to deploy slowly, assume agents will misbehave, and prioritize reversibility over efficiency.

pull_quote.text: Organisations should assume that agentic AI systems may behave unexpectedly and plan deployments accordingly, prioritising resilience, reversibility and risk containment over efficiency gains.
pull_quote.attribution: Careful Adoption of Agentic AI Services

stories.article_excerpt: The cyber agencies of all five Five Eyes nations published their first joint guidance on agentic AI, warning autonomous systems create a new attack surface.

stories.headline: Jamf Ships AI Governance for Mac Fleets, Taking Aim at 'Shadow AI' in the Enterprise
stories.key_takeaway: Jamf's new AI Governance feature, generally available July 1, gives IT teams native, OS-level visibility and policy control over which AI tools run on managed Macs - turning unmonitored 'shadow AI' into an auditable layer and staking out governance as the next enterprise-AI battleground.

pull_quote.text: AI adoption across the enterprise is moving faster than existing technology policies can keep up. Organizations need governance that matches the way AI tools actually operate on Mac.

data_points.label: of workers use unsanctioned AI tools on corporate devices
average added cost of a shadow-AI-linked breach (IBM)

stories.article_excerpt: Jamf shipped AI Governance for Mac fleets, a native control plane that discovers, enforces and audits AI-tool use - a direct answer to 'shadow AI'.

stories.headline: The AI Copyright Case That Could Reshape Fair Use Is Now in the Third Circuit's Hands
stories.key_takeaway: The Third Circuit's pending ruling in Thomson Reuters v. ROSS will be the first appellate decision on whether copying copyrighted work to train an AI is fair use, and its reasoning on the transformative and market-harm factors could steer every generative-AI copyright case now in the pipeline.

pull_quote.text: The question has legal language in it, and what we have taught this machine now is how to think like a lawyer.
pull_quote.role: White & Case partner, arguing for ROSS Intelligence

data_points.label: Share of Westlaw's 28M headnotes ROSS says it copied
Date of Third Circuit oral argument

stories.article_excerpt: For the first time, a federal appeals court is weighing whether copying copyrighted material to train an AI counts as fair use - with the whole industry watching.
